Package org.apache.logging.log4j.spi

Interface TraceContextProvider

public interface TraceContextProvider
Service Provider Interface (SPI) for retrieving distributed tracing metadata (such as W3C Trace Context) from the active execution context.

Implementing this SPI allows tracing frameworks (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Micrometer, Zipkin) to pass native trace identifiers directly to Log4j events. This completely bypasses the ThreadContext map, eliminating map-cloning and garbage collection overhead during asynchronous logging.

Log4j locates implementations of this interface using the standard Java ServiceLoader mechanism. To register a custom provider, create a plain-text file named org.apache.logging.log4j.spi.TraceContextProvider in the META-INF/services/ directory containing the fully qualified class name of the implementation.

Since:
2.27.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getSpanId()
    Returns the standard span ID from the active context, or null.
    String
    getTraceFlags()
    Returns the standard trace flags from the active context, or null.
    String
    getTraceId()
    Returns the standard trace ID from the active context, or null.
